lines.english: Why did the writer complain to the people behind him?
lines.chinese: 作者为什么要向他身后的人抱怨？
lines.raw: Why did the writer complain to the people behind him? | 作者为什么要向他身后的人抱怨？

lines.english: Last week I went to the theatre.
lines.raw: Last week I went to the theatre. | 上周我去了剧院。

lines.english: I had a very good seat.
lines.raw: I had a very good seat. | 我有一个很好的座位。
The play was very interesting. | 戏很有趣。
I did not enjoy it. | 我却没有享受到乐趣。

lines.english: A young man and a young woman were sitting behind me.
lines.chinese: 一对年轻的男女坐在我身后。
lines.raw: A young man and a young woman were sitting behind me. | 一对年轻的男女坐在我身后。
They were talking loudly. | 他们大声地说着话。
I got very angry. | 我非常生气。

lines.english: I could not hear the actors.
lines.raw: I could not hear the actors. | 我听不见演员的讲话。
I turned round. | 我转过身。

lines.english: I looked at the man and the woman angrily.
lines.chinese: 我怒气冲冲地看着那一男一女。
lines.raw: I looked at the man and the woman angrily. | 我怒气冲冲地看着那一男一女。

lines.english: They did not pay any attention.
lines.raw: They did not pay any attention. | 他们却不理不睬。

lines.english: In the end, I could not bear it.
lines.raw: In the end, I could not bear it. | 最后，我忍不住了。
I turned round again. | 我又转过身去。

lines.english: 'I can't hear a word!' I said angrily.
lines.chinese: “我一个字也听不见了！”我生气地说。
lines.raw: 'I can't hear a word!' I said angrily. | “我一个字也听不见了！”我生气地说。
'It's none of your business,' | “不关你的事，”
the young man said rudely. | 那个年轻人粗鲁地说。
'This is a private conversation!' | “这是私人谈话！”
